content.text:
<user_query>
Give me a command to generate an SSH key.Terminal.
</user_query>

Use this in Terminal (recommended `ed25519` key type):

```bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com"
```

If you want it saved to the default path, just press Enter when prompted (`~/.ssh/id_ed25519`).  
Then add it to the ssh-agent on macOS:

```bash
ssh-add --apple-use-keychain ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
```

And copy the public key:

```bash
pbcopy < ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```

**Providing SSH key command**

Use this exact command:

```bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "HUH8@xiaopeng.com"
```

Then (optional but recommended on macOS):

```bash
ssh-add --apple-use-keychain ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
pbcopy < ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```
